source: VEKA/Vlaanderen long-term renovation strategy: existing dwellings must be renovated at an average rate of 3%/yr to EPC label A. The 0.6-1.0%/yr current-rate range is used only for the slow/current scenario; high is an accelerated scenario.
renovation_rate_scenario_meaning: low = lower current estimate; central = policy-compatible; high = accelerated

source: Walloon long-term renovation strategy: residential stock should tend by 2050 towards average decarbonised PEB label A and prioritise deep renovation of least-performing dwellings. Low 0.8%/yr is a conservative current-rate proxy below 1%, not a measured exact value. Central 3%/yr is a policy-compatible assumption aligned with renovation-rate literature and policy discussion pointing to roughly 2.5-3%/yr needs.
current_rate: current-rate proxy 0.8%/yr below 1.0%; not a measured exact value
policy_target_note: 85 kWh/m²·year is the upper threshold of PEB label A / qZEN-level performance; the regional 2050 target is phrased as average decarbonised PEB label A, not merely a numerical energy-score target.
renovation_rate_scenario_meaning: low = conservative current-rate proxy below 1%; central = policy-compatible assumption; high = accelerated

source: Brussels RENOLUTION: by 2050 average residential PEB should be C+ = 100 kWh/m2.year. Official material mainly states that the current rhythm is insufficient; a secondary 0.8%/yr estimate is used as the low/current proxy. Central 2.5%/yr is a cautious policy-compatible scenario; high is accelerated.
current_rate: insufficient; secondary estimate 0.8%/yr used as current-rate proxy
renovation_rate_scenario_meaning: low = secondary current-rate estimate; central = conservative policy-compatible assumption; high = accelerated
